Toss Update: LSG won the toss and will bowl first! 



SPOTLIGHT PERFORMERS

Abhishek Sharma has enjoyed notable success against Lucknow Super Giants, scoring 160 runs in 5 innings at an average of 40 and a blistering strike rate of 229, including two half-centuries. Meanwhile, Aiden Markram has been in exceptional form in 2026, amassing 652 runs in 17 innings at an average of 47 and a strike rate of 162, registering five fifties and a century — six scores of fifty or more — underlining his consistency and impact with the bat.
 

 

PLAYER BATTLES TO WATCH OUT FOR 

A few key individual battles could play a decisive role in shaping the contest. Ishan Kishan has enjoyed a dominant run against Mohammad Shami, putting together 57 runs from 51 balls at a strike rate of 112, with Shami yet to get him out. On the other hand, the matchup between Rishabh Pant and Jaydev Unadkat has been more evenly contested — Pant has scored 49 runs off 31 balls at a strike rate of 158, but Unadkat has managed to dismiss him twice.
 




HEAD TO HEAD:

While Lucknow Super Giants hold the overall edge in this fixture, recent trends tilt slightly in favour of Sunrisers Hyderabad, who have won the last meeting and two of the previous three encounters.

 

MATCH PREVIEW 

The first game of Sunday’s double-header shifts focus to the Orange Army as Sunrisers Hyderabad return to familiar surroundings for their first home fixture of the season at Uppal. They take on the Lucknow Super Giants in what promises to be an intriguing contest.

Sunrisers Hyderabad head into this clash with renewed confidence after a spirited win in Kolkata. In doing so, they became the first side this season to successfully defend a total, bouncing back strongly from their opening defeat. With a formidable top order featuring Abhishek Sharma, Travis Head, and skipper Ishan Kishan, the hosts will be eager to build momentum and kickstart a winning streak in front of their home crowd.

Lucknow Super Giants, on the other hand, have questions to address following a disappointing start with the bat in their opening loss, where they were restricted to 141. A key talking point remains the batting order, particularly the decision to have captain Rishabh Pant open alongside Mitchell Marsh. For Lucknow to challenge effectively, their overseas core of Marsh, Aiden Markram, and Nicholas Pooran will need to step up. Their pace attack, however, showed promise in the previous outing and could play a crucial role once again.

With both teams looking to assert themselves early in the tournament, this contest sets up as a compelling start to Sunday’s action.
MatchSummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Mohammed Shami (Player of the Match):
"I just tried to do my job and I am happy because after losing the first game it was important for us to win and regain some momentum. If you truly love cricket and want to play at a high level then working hard is non negotiable and fitness helps you execute your skills better. Playing domestic cricket helped me find my rhythm again and get into the right flow. I had played at this ground last year and knew that slower balls could be effective here so that was part of the plan. It feels good when the hard work comes together and contributes to a team win."

Played with fire ???????? pic.twitter.com/sToiZU8CGc

— Lucknow Super Giants (@LucknowIPL) April 5, 2026



Rishabh Pant (Lucknow Super Giants Captain):

"It is a big positive to stay till the end and finish the game because that is what wins you matches. The bowlers deserve a lot of credit as they set the game up beautifully with contributions from Shami, Avesh, Rathi and Siddharth. We know there is never a perfect match, so as a management we have to stay critical while also appreciating the effort. The focus is always on execution and learning from every game. Personally I feel well prepared and I want my bat to do the talking because the people around me know how hard I work and that is what really matters."
 

Ishan Kishan (Sunrisers Hyderabad Captain):
"It was a very competitive game and although the total was short, our bowlers did an outstanding job by sticking to the plans and hitting the right areas, with great support from the fielders as well. Losing early wickets in the powerplay always puts pressure on the rest of the batting unit and that is something we need to address because it makes the chase simpler for the opposition. Credit must go to Klaasen and Nitish for batting exactly as the situation demanded and taking the score as deep as possible. We did plan to get Rishabh’s wicket early but he played smartly, nudged singles and built the partnership that was required, which is what good chasers do. Bowling Harshal for those overs was a calculated call and he executed really well, but when margins are small, a couple of boundaries can change everything, and despite the loss taking it to the final over gives us positives to carry forward."


Lucknow Super Giants completed a composed chase at Hyderabad to defeat Sunrisers Hyderabad by five wickets, after controlling the game through discipline with the ball and a calm finish with the bat.

Sunrisers were put under pressure early after opting to bat. Mohammed Shami produced a superb opening spell, finishing with 2 for 9 from four overs and reducing the hosts to 8 for 2 inside three overs. At 26 for 4 in the eighth over, Hyderabad were struggling before Heinrich Klaasen and Nitish Kumar Reddy mounted a strong recovery. Klaasen struck a fluent 62 from 41 balls, while Nitish added power and intent with 56 from 33, the pair adding 116 runs for the fifth wicket and lifting the total to a competitive 156 for 9. Prince Yadav and Avesh Khan supported Shami well, combining for four wickets and keeping the run rate in check.

Lucknow’s chase was built on stability rather than speed. Aiden Markram gave early impetus with 45 from 27 balls, taking the score to 77 by the ninth over. Rishabh Pant then anchored the innings with an unbeaten 68 from 50 deliveries, striking nine boundaries and absorbing pressure through the middle. Despite brief stutters that left Lucknow at 139 for 5 in the eighteenth over, Pant held firm to guide the side to 160 for 5 in 19.5 overs..
